questions.question: Explain why Bidirectional RNNs are particularly useful for Named Entity Recognition tasks.
expectedPoints: NER requires contextual understanding from both sides of a word
Example: 'Apple' could be company or fruit depending on context
Bi-RNNs capture both past and future context
This leads to more accurate entity classification

questions.question: Compare and contrast unidirectional and bidirectional RNNs, discussing their architectures, applications, and limitations.
expectedPoints: Architecture: Uni-RNNs process one direction, Bi-RNNs process both
Context access: Uni-RNNs only past, Bi-RNNs past and future
Real-time applicability: Uni-RNNs suitable, Bi-RNNs not suitable
Performance: Bi-RNNs superior for context-dependent tasks
Memory requirements: Bi-RNNs require more memory
